ex.en: The painter finished the portrait in a week.
ex.ko: 그 화가는 일주일 만에 초상화를 완성했다.

painter.en: a worker whose job is to cover walls or objects with paint

ex.en: We hired a painter to repaint the fence.
ex.ko: 우리는 울타리를 다시 칠하려고 페인트공을 고용했다.

ex.en: We've been pals since childhood.

pan.en: a wide metal container used for cooking food

ex.en: Heat some oil in the pan.

panic.en: suddenly feel so frightened that you cannot think clearly

ex.en: Don't panic, everything will be fine.

panic.en: a sudden overwhelming feeling of fear

ex.en: There was panic in the crowd when the alarm went off.
ex.ko: 경보가 울리자 군중 사이에 공황이 일었다.

part.en: leave someone or stop being together

ex.en: The two friends parted at the station.

part.en: a piece or section of something that combines with other pieces to make a whole

ex.en: This is the best part of the movie.
ex.ko: 이게 그 영화에서 가장 좋은 부분이야.

part.en: a piece that is used to make a machine or object

ex.en: We need to order some car parts.
ex.ko: 우리는 자동차 부품을 좀 주문해야 한다.

part.en: the character that an actor plays in a film or play

ex.en: She played the part of the queen.

partly.en: to some degree, but not completely

ex.en: The delay was partly due to bad weather.
ex.ko: 그 지연은 부분적으로 나쁜 날씨 때문이었다.

pass.en: move past or through a place

ex.en: We passed a small village on the way.
ex.ko: 우리는 가는 길에 작은 마을을 지나쳤다.

pass.en: give something to someone by putting it in their hand

ex.en: Could you pass the salt, please?

pass.en: succeed in a test or examination

ex.en: She passed her driving test on the first try.
ex.ko: 그녀는 첫 시도에 운전면허 시험에 합격했다.

pass.en: a card or document that allows you to enter a place or use a service

ex.en: You need a pass to enter the stadium.
Two years passed quickly.

passage.en: a short section of a piece of writing

ex.en: Read the passage and answer the questions.

passage.en: a long narrow area that connects one place to another

ex.en: They walked down a narrow passage to the exit.
ex.ko: 그들은 좁은 통로를 걸어 출구로 향했다.

passenger.en: a person traveling in a vehicle who is not driving or operating it

ex.en: The bus can carry fifty passengers.
ex.ko: 그 버스는 승객 50명을 태울 수 있다.

pasta.en: an Italian food made from flour and water, often served with sauce

ex.en: We had pasta for dinner.
We followed the path through the forest.

path.en: a course of action that leads towards a particular result

ex.en: She chose a difficult path to success.
ex.ko: 그녀는 성공으로 가는 어려운 길을 택했다.

patient.en: able to wait or accept problems without becoming annoyed

ex.en: Please be patient; the bus will come soon.
ex.ko: 조금만 참으세요, 버스는 곧 올 거예요.

patient.en: a person who is receiving medical treatment

ex.en: The doctor examined the patient carefully.

PC.en: a personal computer, especially one used at home or in an office

ex.en: He bought a new PC for gaming.

pea.en: a small round green vegetable that grows in pods

ex.en: She added peas to the soup.

peaceful.en: calm and free from disturbance or war

ex.en: It was a peaceful morning in the village.

peaceful.en: not using violence, especially in a protest

ex.en: They organized a peaceful protest.

pear.en: a sweet juicy fruit that is narrow at the top and round at the bottom

ex.en: She sliced a ripe pear for the salad.
ex.ko: 그녀는 샐러드를 위해 잘 익은 배를 썰었다.

pence.en: the plural of penny, a small unit of British money

ex.en: The chocolate bar costs eighty pence.

penfriend.en: someone you become friends with by writing letters, usually without meeting

ex.en: I have a penfriend in France.

pepper.en: a spicy powder used to flavor food, made from dried berries

ex.en: Add some salt and pepper to the soup.

pepper.en: a hollow vegetable, often red, green, or yellow, that can be mild or spicy

ex.en: She chopped a red pepper for the salad.

perfect.en: make something completely correct or as good as it can be

ex.en: She practiced for years to perfect her technique.
ex.ko: 그녀는 기술을 완벽하게 다듬기 위해 수년간 연습했다.

perfect.en: having no faults or flaws; as good as possible

ex.en: It was a perfect day for a picnic.

perfectly.en: in a way that has no faults; completely

ex.en: She sang the song perfectly.

perform.en: entertain an audience by singing, acting, or playing music

ex.en: The band performed at the festival.

perform.en: carry out a task or action

ex.en: The surgeon performed the operation successfully.

performance.en: a show performed in front of an audience

ex.en: We watched an amazing dance performance.

performance.en: how well someone or something does a task

ex.en: The team's performance improved this season.
ex.ko: 그 팀의 성과는 이번 시즌에 향상되었다.

perfume.en: a liquid with a pleasant smell that people put on their skin

ex.en: She wore a light floral perfume.

perhaps.en: used to say that something is possible but not certain

ex.en: Perhaps we should leave earlier.
ex.ko: 아마도 우리는 더 일찍 출발해야 할 것 같다.

permission.en: official agreement that lets you do something

ex.en: You need permission to park here.

personality.en: the set of qualities that make a person different from others

ex.en: She has a warm and friendly personality.
ex.ko: 그녀는 따뜻하고 친근한 성격을 가지고 있다.

personality.en: a person who is well known, especially in entertainment or media

ex.en: He became a famous TV personality.

petrol.en: fuel used in cars and other vehicles (British English)

ex.en: We stopped to buy some petrol.

petrol station.en: a place where you can buy petrol for your car

ex.en: There's a petrol station just around the corner.

photograph.en: take a picture of someone or something using a camera

ex.en: He photographed the sunset.

photograph.en: a picture made using a camera

ex.en: She showed us photographs from her trip.
ex.ko: 그녀는 여행에서 찍은 사진들을 우리에게 보여주었다.

photographer.en: someone whose job is taking photographs

ex.en: The photographer took stunning pictures of the wedding.
ex.ko: 그 사진작가는 결혼식의 멋진 사진을 찍었다.

photography.en: the activity or skill of taking photographs

ex.en: She studied photography in college.

physical.en: relating to the body rather than the mind

ex.en: Regular exercise is good for your physical health.

physical.en: relating to things that can be seen or touched, or to the laws of nature

ex.en: The physical world is governed by laws of science.
ex.ko: 물리적 세계는 과학 법칙의 지배를 받는다.

physically.en: in a way that relates to the body

ex.en: He felt physically exhausted after the marathon.
ex.ko: 그는 마라톤을 뛰고 나서 신체적으로 지쳤다.

pile.en: a number of things stacked on top of each other

ex.en: There was a pile of books on the desk.

pile.en: put things one on top of another to form a pile

ex.en: Dirty dishes piled up in the sink.

pilgrim.en: a person who travels to a sacred place for religious reasons

ex.en: Pilgrims travel to Mecca every year.

pill.en: a small round piece of medicine that you swallow

ex.en: Take one pill after each meal.
ex.ko: 매 식사 후에 알약 하나를 드세요.

pilot.en: a person who is trained and licensed to fly an aircraft

ex.en: The pilot announced a delay before takeoff.

pilot.en: a small trial version of something done before starting it fully

ex.en: They launched a pilot program in three schools.
ex.ko: 그들은 세 개 학교에서 시범 프로그램을 시작했다.

pity.en: a feeling of sadness for someone else's suffering

ex.en: She felt pity for the homeless man.

pity.en: an unfortunate situation that causes disappointment

ex.en: It's a pity you can't come to the party.

planet.en: a large round object in space that moves around a star such as the sun

ex.en: Earth is the third planet from the sun.

plant.en: put a seed or young plant into the ground to grow

ex.en: We planted tomatoes in the garden.

plant.en: a living thing that grows in the ground and has leaves and roots

ex.en: She waters the plants every morning.
ex.ko: 그녀는 매일 아침 식물에 물을 준다.

plant.en: a large building where goods are manufactured

ex.en: The company opened a new manufacturing plant.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 새 제조 공장을 열었다.

plastic.en: a light, strong material made artificially, used to make many objects

ex.en: The toy is made of plastic.

plate.en: a flat round dish used for eating food from

ex.en: He put the sandwich on a plate.

plate.en: a flat, thin sheet of metal, glass, or another hard material

ex.en: The window was covered with a metal plate.

playful.en: full of fun and enjoying playing

ex.en: The puppy has a playful personality.
ex.ko: 그 강아지는 장난기 많은 성격을 가지고 있다.

playground.en: an outdoor area where children can play, often with swings and slides

ex.en: The kids ran to the playground after school.
We had a pleasant evening together.

pleased.en: feeling happy or satisfied about something

ex.en: I'm pleased with my exam results.

pleasing.en: giving pleasure or satisfaction, especially to look at or listen to

ex.en: The garden has a pleasing view of the lake.
ex.ko: 그 정원은 호수의 만족스러운 경치를 갖고 있다.

plenty.en: a large amount that is more than enough

ex.en: There's plenty of food for everyone.

plural.en: the form of a word used when referring to more than one

ex.en: The plural of 'child' is 'children'.

plus.en: a good or useful feature of something

ex.en: Being bilingual is a big plus for this job.
ex.ko: 이중언어를 구사하는 것은 이 일에 큰 장점이다.

plus.en: used to show that one number is being added to another

ex.en: Two plus two equals four.

police.en: the official organization responsible for keeping order and enforcing the law

ex.en: The police arrived quickly after the accident.

police officer.en: a member of the police force

ex.en: A police officer helped the lost child find her parents.
ex.ko: 한 경찰관이 길 잃은 아이가 부모를 찾도록 도왔다.

police station.en: a building where police officers work

ex.en: He went to the police station to report the theft.
ex.ko: 그는 도난 신고를 하러 경찰서에 갔다.

policeman.en: a male member of the police force

ex.en: The policeman directed traffic at the intersection.

policewoman.en: a female member of the police force

ex.en: The policewoman calmed the frightened witness.
ex.ko: 그 여자 경찰관은 겁에 질린 목격자를 진정시켰다.

polite.en: showing good manners and respect for other people

ex.en: It's polite to say thank you.

political.en: relating to politics or the government

ex.en: The country is facing a political crisis.
ex.ko: 그 나라는 정치적 위기에 직면해 있다.

pollute.en: make air, water, or land dirty and harmful

ex.en: Factories pollute the river with waste.

popular.en: liked or enjoyed by many people

ex.en: This song became popular overnight.
ex.ko: 이 노래는 하룻밤 사이에 인기를 얻었다.

popular.en: relating to or intended for ordinary people in general

ex.en: The government increased popular support by cutting taxes.
ex.ko: 정부는 세금을 낮춰 대중적 지지를 높였다.

population.en: the number of people living in a particular place

ex.en: The population of the city has grown rapidly.

portrait.en: a painting, drawing, or photograph of a person

ex.en: The artist painted a portrait of the king.

position.en: the place where someone or something is

ex.en: Mark the position of the treasure on the map.

position.en: a job or role within an organization

ex.en: She applied for a position at the bank.

position.en: the way someone's body is arranged

ex.en: He changed his sleeping position.

position.en: an opinion or attitude about something

ex.en: What is your position on this issue?
ex.ko: 이 문제에 대한 당신의 입장은 무엇인가요?

possible.en: able to happen, exist, or be done

ex.en: Is it possible to finish this by tomorrow?

possibly.en: used to say something might be true or might happen

ex.en: We could possibly meet next week.
ex.ko: 우리는 아마도 다음 주에 만날 수 있을 거야.

post office.en: a place where you can buy stamps and send letters or packages

ex.en: I need to go to the post office to mail this package.
ex.ko: 이 소포를 보내려면 우체국에 가야 한다.

pour.en: make a liquid flow from a container

ex.en: She poured water into the glasses.
It poured all afternoon.

power.en: the ability to do something or make things happen

ex.en: Knowledge is power.

power.en: control or authority over people or events

ex.en: The new president has a lot of power.
ex.ko: 새 대통령은 많은 권력을 가지고 있다.

power.en: energy, especially electricity, used to make things work

ex.en: The storm cut off the power to our house.

powerful.en: having great strength, force, or influence

ex.en: The company has a powerful influence on the market.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 시장에 강력한 영향력을 가지고 있다.

ex.en: I need the precise measurements of the room.

predict.en: say what you think will happen in the future

ex.en: Scientists predict that sea levels will rise.

prediction.en: a statement about what will happen in the future

ex.en: His prediction about the election turned out to be correct.
ex.ko: 선거에 대한 그의 예측은 맞는 것으로 판명되었다.

prefer.en: like one thing better than another

ex.en: I prefer tea to coffee.

prepare.en: get ready for something in advance

ex.en: We need to prepare for the exam.
She prepared dinner for the guests.

pressure.en: the force produced by pressing on something

ex.en: Check the tire pressure before you drive.

pressure.en: a feeling of stress caused by difficulties or demands

ex.en: She's under a lot of pressure at work.
ex.ko: 그녀는 직장에서 큰 압박을 받고 있다.

pretend.en: not real, but imagined or imitated, especially in children's play

ex.en: The kids had a pretend tea party.

pretend.en: behave as if something is true when it is not

ex.en: He pretended to be asleep.

ex.ko: 규칙적인 운동은 많은 질병을 예방할 수 있다.

pride.en: a feeling of pleasure or satisfaction from your own achievements, qualities, or possessions

ex.en: She takes great pride in her work.
ex.ko: 그녀는 자신의 일에 큰 자부심을 느낀다.

principal.en: the original amount of money that was borrowed or invested, not including interest

ex.en: He paid off the principal but still owes interest.
ex.ko: 그는 원금은 갚았지만 아직 이자가 남아 있다.

principal.en: the person in charge of a school

ex.en: The principal gave a speech at the assembly.
The principal reason for the delay was bad weather.

print.en: produce text or pictures on paper using a machine

ex.en: Please print the document for me.
ex.ko: 그 문서를 저를 위해 인쇄해 주세요.

print.en: text or images that have been printed

ex.en: The print was too small to read.
ex.ko: 그 글자가 너무 작아서 읽을 수가 없었다.

printer.en: a machine that prints documents or pictures

ex.en: The printer is out of paper.

private.en: belonging to or concerning one particular person, not for public use

ex.en: This is a private conversation.

pro.en: a person who does something as their profession, especially in sports (informal)

ex.en: She turned pro at the age of eighteen.

probably.en: used to say that something is likely to be true

ex.en: It will probably rain tomorrow.

produce.en: make or create something, especially in large quantities

ex.en: The factory produces thousands of cars each year.
ex.ko: 그 공장은 매년 수천 대의 자동차를 생산한다.

produce.en: fresh fruits and vegetables sold in shops or markets

ex.en: The store sells fresh local produce.
ex.ko: 그 가게는 신선한 지역 농산물을 판다.

product.en: something that is made to be sold

ex.en: This is our best-selling product.
ex.ko: 이것은 우리의 가장 잘 팔리는 제품이다.

production.en: the process of making or growing something, especially in large quantities

ex.en: The factory increased its production this year.

production.en: a play, film, or show that is performed or made

ex.en: The theater is putting on a new production of Hamlet.
ex.ko: 그 극장은 '햄릿'의 새로운 공연을 올리고 있다.

professional.en: a person who has a job that requires special training or skill

ex.en: You should see a professional about that problem.

professional.en: relating to or suitable for a profession

ex.en: She gave a very professional presentation.

progressive.en: supporting new ideas, change, and reform, especially in politics or society

ex.en: The city has a progressive mayor.
ex.ko: 그 도시는 진보적인 시장을 두고 있다.

progressive.en: happening or developing gradually over time

ex.en: The disease causes progressive muscle weakness.
ex.ko: 그 질병은 점진적인 근력 약화를 일으킨다.

promise.en: say that you will definitely do something

ex.en: He promised to call her every day.

promise.en: a statement that you will definitely do something

ex.en: She kept her promise to help him.

promise.en: signs that someone or something will be successful in the future

ex.en: The young player shows great promise.
ex.ko: 그 젊은 선수는 큰 장래성을 보여준다.

pronounce.en: say a word or sound in a particular way

ex.en: How do you pronounce this word?

pronunciation.en: the way a word or language is spoken

ex.en: Her pronunciation of English is very clear.

proper.en: right, appropriate, or correct for a particular situation

ex.en: Make sure you wear the proper clothes for the interview.

provide.en: give someone something that they need

ex.en: The hotel provides free breakfast.

psychologist.en: a scientist who studies the human mind and behavior

ex.en: She sees a psychologist once a week.
ex.ko: 그녀는 일주일에 한 번 심리학자를 만난다.

public.en: provided for or used by people in general, not privately owned

ex.en: This is a public park.
The museum is open to the public.

publish.en: prepare and print a book, magazine, or article for people to buy or read

ex.en: The author published her first novel last year.
ex.ko: 그 작가는 작년에 첫 소설을 출판했다.

pull.en: use force to move something toward yourself

ex.en: Pull the door to open it.

punctuation.en: the marks (such as commas and periods) used in writing to separate sentences and clauses

ex.en: Punctuation makes writing easier to understand.
ex.ko: 문장부호는 글을 더 이해하기 쉽게 만든다.

purpose.en: the reason for doing something or the aim you want to achieve

ex.en: The purpose of the meeting is to discuss the budget.
ex.ko: 그 회의의 목적은 예산을 논의하는 것이다.

pursue.en: try to achieve or gain something over a period of time

ex.en: She decided to pursue a career in medicine.
ex.ko: 그녀는 의학 분야의 경력을 추구하기로 결심했다.

pursue.en: follow someone or something in order to catch them

ex.en: The police pursued the stolen car through the city.
ex.ko: 경찰은 도난 차량을 도시 곳곳에서 추격했다.

quality.en: of a high standard or grade

ex.en: This store sells quality furniture.

quality.en: how good or bad something is

ex.en: The quality of this fabric is excellent.

quality.en: a characteristic or feature that someone or something has

ex.en: Patience is an important quality for a teacher.

queen.en: a woman who rules a country, or the wife of a king

ex.en: The queen visited the hospital yesterday.

queen.en: the most powerful piece in chess

ex.en: She moved her queen across the board.

quick.en: done or happening in a short time

ex.en: Let's take a quick break.
She closed the door quietly.

quit.en: stop doing something, especially a habit or activity

ex.en: He quit smoking last year.
She quit her job to travel the world.
ex.ko: 그녀는 세계를 여행하기 위해 직장을 그만뒀다.

quite.en: to a fairly large degree; considerably

ex.en: The movie was quite interesting.

quiz.en: a short test or a game of answering questions

ex.en: We have a vocabulary quiz tomorrow.

racket.en: a sports tool with a handle and a frame of strings, used to hit a ball

ex.en: She bought a new tennis racket.
The children were making a terrible racket upstairs.
ex.ko: 아이들이 위층에서 끔찍한 소음을 내고 있었다.

raincoat.en: a coat that keeps you dry in the rain

ex.en: Don't forget your raincoat, it might rain today.
ex.ko: 비가 올지도 모르니 우비를 잊지 마.

raise.en: lift something to a higher position

ex.en: Raise your hand if you know the answer.

raise.en: look after a child until they become an adult

ex.en: They raised three children in this house.
ex.ko: 그들은 이 집에서 세 아이를 길렀다.

raise.en: collect money for a particular purpose

ex.en: The school is raising money for new books.
ex.ko: 그 학교는 새 책을 위한 기금을 모으고 있다.

raise.en: an increase in salary or pay (especially American English)

ex.en: She got a raise after her promotion.
ex.ko: 그녀는 승진 후 임금 인상을 받았다.

ex.en: Prices range from ten to fifty dollars.

range.en: the area of variation between limits on a particular scale

ex.en: This job has a wide range of responsibilities.
ex.ko: 이 일은 다양한 범위의 책임을 포함한다.

range.en: a series of hills or mountains

ex.en: The Rocky Mountains form a huge mountain range.

rap.en: hit something quickly and lightly, making a short sound

ex.en: She rapped on the window to get his attention.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그의 주의를 끌려고 창문을 톡톡 두드렸다.

rap.en: a type of music in which words are spoken in rhythm to a beat

ex.en: He loves listening to rap music.
ex.ko: 그는 랩 음악 듣는 것을 좋아한다.

rate.en: give a rank or score to something based on its quality

ex.en: Critics rated the film highly.

rate.en: a measure of how fast something happens or how much of something there is compared to something else

ex.en: The birth rate has dropped in recent years.

rate.en: a fixed price charged for something

ex.en: What's the hourly rate for this service?

rather.en: instead of what was expected or mentioned

ex.en: I'd like tea rather than coffee.
ex.ko: 저는 커피보다 오히려 차를 마시고 싶어요.

ex.en: The weather was rather cold yesterday.

rating.en: a measurement of how good or popular something is

ex.en: The restaurant has a high customer rating.
Sushi is made with raw fish.

raw.en: in its natural state, not yet processed or manufactured

ex.en: Raw materials are shipped to the factory for processing.
A ray of sunlight came through the window.
ex.ko: 한 줄기 햇빛이 창문을 통해 들어왔다.

reach.en: the distance that someone or something can extend or be extended to

ex.en: The top shelf is out of my reach.
ex.ko: 맨 위 선반은 내 손이 닿지 않는다.

reach.en: arrive at a place after traveling

ex.en: We reached the airport just in time.

reach.en: stretch out your hand or arm to touch or get something

ex.en: She reached for the top shelf.
ex.ko: 그녀는 맨 위 선반으로 손을 뻗었다.

reach.en: succeed in contacting someone, especially by phone

ex.en: I couldn't reach him on the phone.
ex.ko: 나는 그에게 전화로 연락이 닿지 않았다.

realize.en: become aware of or understand something

ex.en: I suddenly realized I had left my keys at home.
ex.ko: 나는 갑자기 열쇠를 집에 두고 왔다는 것을 깨달았다.

receipt.en: a piece of paper that shows you have paid for something

ex.en: Keep the receipt in case you need to return the item.
ex.ko: 물건을 반품해야 할 경우를 대비해 영수증을 보관해라.

receive.en: get something that is given or sent to you

ex.en: She received a letter from her friend.

recent.en: happening or made a short time ago

ex.en: Have you seen his most recent film?
I recently moved to a new apartment.

receptionist.en: a person whose job is to answer the phone and greet visitors at an office or hotel

ex.en: The receptionist gave us our room key.

record.en: information kept in written or other permanent form

ex.en: Keep a record of your expenses.

record.en: store sound or images using electronic equipment

ex.en: She recorded the meeting on her phone.

recycle.en: process used materials so they can be used again

ex.en: We recycle paper, glass, and plastic.
She referred to the report in her speech.
Please refer to the manual for instructions.

refer.en: send someone to another person or place for help or information

ex.en: The doctor referred her to a specialist.

reflect.en: show or be a sign of something

ex.en: Her choice of words reflects her feelings.
ex.ko: 그녀의 단어 선택은 그녀의 감정을 반영한다.

reflect.en: throw back light, heat, or an image from a surface

ex.en: The lake reflected the mountains perfectly.
She took a moment to reflect on her decision.
ex.ko: 그녀는 잠시 자신의 결정에 대해 숙고했다.

refrigerator.en: an appliance used to keep food cold

ex.en: Put the milk back in the refrigerator.

regret.en: a feeling of sadness about something you did or did not do

ex.en: She has no regrets about her decision.
ex.ko: 그녀는 자신의 결정에 대해 후회가 없다.

regret.en: feel sorry about something you did or did not do

ex.en: I regret not studying harder in school.
ex.ko: 나는 학교에서 더 열심히 공부하지 않은 것을 후회한다.

regular.en: happening often, at fixed times, or in a fixed pattern

ex.en: Regular exercise keeps you healthy.
He visits his grandmother regularly.

relax.en: become calm and free from stress or tension

ex.en: I like to relax by reading a book.
ex.ko: 나는 책을 읽으며 휴식을 취하는 걸 좋아한다.

relaxed.en: calm and free from worry or tension

ex.en: She felt relaxed after the massage.
ex.ko: 그녀는 마사지를 받은 후 편안함을 느꼈다.

release.en: let go of something or someone you were holding

ex.en: The zookeeper released the bird into the wild.

release.en: make something available to the public for the first time

ex.en: The band released a new album this week.
ex.ko: 그 밴드는 이번 주 새 앨범을 발매했다.

release.en: a new product, movie, or piece of music made available to the public

ex.en: This is the band's latest release.

remain.en: stay in the same place or condition

ex.en: She remained calm during the emergency.
Please remind me to call him later.

remote.en: far away from cities or other places where people live

ex.en: They live in a remote village in the mountains.

remote.en: a device used to control a machine such as a TV from a distance

ex.en: Where is the TV remote?

rent.en: money you pay regularly to use a house, room, or property

ex.en: The rent for this apartment is expensive.

rent.en: pay to use something that belongs to someone else

ex.en: We rented a car for the trip.

repair.en: fix something that is broken or damaged

ex.en: He repaired the broken window.

repair.en: the act of fixing something that is broken or damaged

ex.en: The car is in the shop for repairs.
ex.ko: 그 차는 수리를 위해 정비소에 있다.

replace.en: put a new or different thing in place of something old or broken

ex.en: We need to replace the batteries.

reply.en: say or write something as an answer

ex.en: She replied to my email immediately.

reply.en: something said or written as an answer

ex.en: I haven't received a reply yet.

report.en: give a spoken or written account of something

ex.en: She reported the results to her manager.

report.en: a written or spoken account of something, such as an event or a study

ex.en: He wrote a report on climate change.
ex.ko: 그는 기후 변화에 관한 보고서를 작성했다.

report.en: tell the authorities about a crime or problem

ex.en: They reported the theft to the police.

represent.en: act or speak officially for a person or group

ex.en: She will represent our school at the competition.
ex.ko: 그녀는 대회에서 우리 학교를 대표할 것이다.

request.en: ask for something politely or formally

ex.en: He requested more information about the course.
ex.ko: 그는 그 강좌에 대한 추가 정보를 요청했다.

request.en: an act of asking for something politely or formally

ex.en: The teacher granted her request for extra time.
ex.ko: 선생님은 그녀의 추가 시간 요청을 승인했다.

research.en: study a subject carefully to discover new information

ex.en: Scientists are researching new ways to treat cancer.
ex.ko: 과학자들은 암 치료의 새로운 방법을 연구하고 있다.

research.en: the careful study of a subject to discover new information

ex.en: Her research focuses on climate change.
ex.ko: 그녀의 연구는 기후 변화에 초점을 맞추고 있다.

response.en: something said or done as a reaction to something else

ex.en: There was no response to her question.

retire.en: stop working, usually because of age

ex.en: My father retired last year after 40 years of teaching.
ex.ko: 우리 아버지는 40년간 가르친 후 작년에 은퇴하셨다.

retired.en: no longer working, usually because of age

ex.en: My grandfather is a retired doctor.
